domain_order.domain_baseline_risk: approximately 35% 10-year incident type 2 diabetes risk; prediabetes, prior gestational diabetes, Asian ancestry, central adiposity, family history, lower CRF
domain_order.residual_opportunity: high; no diabetes-prevention program/medication documented and several modifiable drivers remain active
domain_order.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
domain_order.why_ordered_here: Largest modeled absolute opportunity and most addressable upstream risk for Karen.

domain_order.domain_baseline_risk: 7.5% 10-year total CVD risk with elevated LDL-C/non-HDL/ApoB, treated borderline BP, family history, prediabetes and lower CRF
domain_order.residual_opportunity: moderate-high; lipid, BP, activity, diet, sleep and alcohol levers remain partially unoptimized
domain_order.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
domain_order.why_ordered_here: Intermediate near-term vascular risk with multiple guideline-grade modifiable levers.

domain_order.domain_baseline_risk: CHEK2 likely pathogenic variant with moderate hereditary breast risk; dense breasts and first-degree breast cancer family history; colorectal start-age requirement satisfied by normal 2024 colonoscopy
domain_order.residual_opportunity: moderate; major opportunity is surveillance gap closure and alcohol reduction rather than a broad active prevention regimen
domain_order.why_ordered_here: Important inherited-risk domain, but active risk-reduction options are narrower than surveillance and metabolic/CVD prevention.

domain_order.domain_baseline_risk: approximately 2.5% 5-year incident CKD risk; eGFR 88 and UACR 12 mg/g currently reassuring
domain_order.residual_opportunity: low-moderate; protect against future diabetes/BP/ACE-inhibitor safety issues
domain_order.why_ordered_here: Current kidney markers are normal; opportunity is mainly safety and prevention of downstream risk conversion.

domain_order.domain_baseline_risk: approximately 4.4% 20-year all-cause dementia risk with sleep efficiency and vascular modifiers
domain_order.residual_opportunity: low-moderate; vascular, sleep, and stress levers overlap with higher-opportunity domains
domain_order.why_ordered_here: Lower absolute modeled risk; most levers are already captured through metabolic/CVD and sleep/stress actions.

domain_order.domain_baseline_risk: general preventive care and medication/supplement safety, including bone and thyroid maintenance
domain_order.residual_opportunity: context-dependent; mainly maintenance or due-overdue monitoring
domain_order.why_ordered_here: Important for care completeness but not the primary absolute risk-opportunity driver.

SoC Monitoring.title: Initiate annual breast MRI surveillance in addition to mammography
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Breast MRI is the clearest unresolved standard surveillance gap in the supplied cancer model: CHEK2 likely pathogenic status maps to annual mammogram plus annual breast MRI from age 40, and her breast MRI is documented as not done.

SoC Monitoring.title: Order baseline DEXA bone density screening
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: The imaging/screening payload states DEXA is not done and notes it is due now given postmenopausal status and age 52.

SoC Monitoring.title: Monitor renal function, albuminuria, and ACE-inhibitor safety labs
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Kidney markers are currently reassuring, but she has hypertension, prediabetes risk, and lisinopril use.

SoC Monitoring.title: Monitor HbA1c and fasting glucose for prediabetes trajectory
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: She has prediabetes-range HbA1c and fasting glucose with high estimated 10-year incident T2D risk.

SoC Monitoring.title: Continue structured home and clinic blood pressure monitoring
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Her hypertension is treated and not uncontrolled, but readings remain slightly above stricter optimal thresholds.

SoC Monitoring.title: Repeat lipid panel with ApoB/non-HDL tracking for CVD risk surveillance
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: She has intermediate estimated CVD risk and atherogenic lipid burden.

SoC Monitoring.title: Use ambulatory blood pressure monitoring or a validated multi-day home BP protocol if control status remains uncertain
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: The domain models flag missing data on BP variability and nocturnal dipping, and available readings are borderline.

SoC Monitoring.title: Consider coronary artery calcium scoring as a standard CVD risk-refinement gate
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: The CVD model reports intermediate 10-year risk and identifies CAC as not done, with CAC unavailable for reclassification.

SoC Monitoring.title: Reconcile CHEK2-specific colorectal surveillance interval after normal 2024 colonoscopy
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Colonoscopy was normal in 2024-06 and the fixture lists next due in 2034, but the cancer model states CHEK2 triggers colonoscopy from age 40 while noting the repeat interval is indeterminate in the supplied specification.

SoC Monitoring.title: Track waist circumference, weight trajectory, and BMI as metabolic surveillance markers
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Despite normal-range BMI, she has waist-to-height ratio above threshold and mild upward weight drift since menopause.

SoC Monitoring.title: Monitor vitamin D status in the context of supplementation and bone-health evaluation
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: She takes vitamin D3 2000 IU daily but 25-OH vitamin D is 28 ng/mL, and DEXA is due.

SoC Monitoring.title: Continue thyroid function monitoring for Hashimoto hypothyroidism on levothyroxine
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Her hypothyroidism is documented as controlled on levothyroxine, with current TSH and free T4 in the supplied labs.

SoC Monitoring.title: Repeat validated monitoring for sleep, stress, anxiety, mood, alcohol use, and OSA risk
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: She has high perceived stress, mild anxiety, threshold AUDIT-C for women, sleep restriction, and poor sleep efficiency, while PHQ-9 and STOP-BANG are currently low.

SoC Monitoring.title: Continue annual screening mammography with documentation of BIRADS and breast density
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Her last mammogram in 2026-01 was BIRADS 2 with annual frequency documented.

SoC Monitoring.title: Maintain cervical cancer screening interval with next Pap/HPV due in 2029
SoC Monitoring.ordering_rationale: Her Pap/HPV test in 2024-09 was negative and HPV negative, with next due listed as 2029.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Enroll in or emulate a structured Diabetes Prevention Program
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: high expected metabolic ARR: DPP-class intervention addresses the dominant ~35% 10-year diabetes opportunity
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Structured lifestyle programs for prediabetes are standard-of-care risk reduction, emphasizing activity, nutrition, weight/waist management, and behavioral support to reduce progression to type 2 diabetes.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Karen has prediabetes-range glycemia plus multiple amplifiers: prior gestational diabetes, Asian ancestry, paternal type 2 diabetes, elevated waist-to-height ratio, and high modeled 10-year diabetes risk.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Prevent further postmenopausal weight gain and reduce central adiposity
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: moderate expected metabolic ARR through central-adiposity and postmenopausal weight-trajectory modification
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: For people with prediabetes or central adiposity, standard lifestyle care supports preventing weight gain and reducing visceral adiposity through nutrition, activity, resistance training, and sleep optimization.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Although BMI is in the normal range, waist-to-height ratio is elevated and weight has drifted upward since menopause.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Shift carbohydrate quality and meal composition for prediabetes
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: moderate expected metabolic ARR through lower postprandial glucose/insulin exposure
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Nutrition counseling for prediabetes commonly emphasizes high-fiber carbohydrates, reduced refined starches and added sugars, protein/fat/fiber pairing, and portion quality.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Karen’s glycemic markers are in the prediabetes range with elevated insulin resistance despite normal BMI.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Add progressive resistance training 2 to 3 days per week
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: moderate expected metabolic ARR through insulin sensitivity, lean mass, and bone/function benefits
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Standard exercise guidance supports muscle-strengthening activity for adults, with particular relevance after menopause.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Resistance training is not listed among her current exercise modalities, which are indoor cycling and hiking.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Progressively increase cardiorespiratory fitness beyond current baseline
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: moderate expected metabolic ARR with cross-domain CVD/neuro co-benefit from improved CRF
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Regular aerobic activity and improved cardiorespiratory fitness are standard risk-reduction measures for cardiometabolic disease and are associated with better vascular and cognitive aging outcomes.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Cardiorespiratory fitness was below average and fired modifiers in the CVD, metabolic, and neuro models.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Clinician shared decision on lipid-lowering therapy for intermediate CVD risk
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: moderate expected CVD ARR from ApoB/LDL-lowering shared decision in intermediate risk
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Guideline-based cardiovascular prevention commonly supports statin consideration for adults with intermediate estimated CVD risk and atherogenic lipid burden after shared decision-making.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Karen has intermediate estimated 10-year total CVD risk with elevated LDL-C, non-HDL-C, ApoB, and modestly elevated Lp(a).

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Optimize blood pressure toward consistently below 130/80 if clinically appropriate
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: moderate expected CVD ARR from moving treated BP consistently below 130/80 if tolerated
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Blood pressure reduction is a standard, guideline-grade intervention for lowering stroke, coronary, kidney, and cognitive vascular risk.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Her BP is treated and not frankly uncontrolled, but home and clinic values remain slightly above stricter optimal thresholds used across CVD, CKD, and neurovascular risk models.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Adopt a lipid-lowering dietary pattern focused on ApoB reduction
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small-moderate expected CVD ARR as diet-mediated ApoB/LDL reduction
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Dietary patterns lower in saturated fat and higher in unsaturated fats and soluble fiber are standard nonpharmacologic therapy for LDL-C and ApoB reduction.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Her atherogenic lipid burden is a major CVD driver.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use a DASH-style sodium-reduction strategy for BP control
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small-moderate expected CVD ARR through BP lowering; complements medication strategy
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: DASH-style eating and sodium reduction are standard nonpharmacologic interventions for lowering blood pressure.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: BP is treated but remains slightly above optimal.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Reduce alcohol intake below current women’s AUDIT-C threshold pattern
SoC Risk Reduction.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small-moderate expected CVD/cancer/metabolic ARR by reducing alcohol exposure at threshold AUDIT-C pattern
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Reducing alcohol is a standard harm-reduction and prevention action when intake is at a screening threshold or interacts with sleep, BP, metabolic risk, or cancer-risk context.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Her alcohol intake is not high enough to fire cancer or neuro alcohol modifiers, but AUDIT-C is at the threshold for women and systemic output notes possible interaction with sleep, BP, glycemia, triglycerides, and breast-risk context.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Implement a sleep-extension and sleep-efficiency improvement plan
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small-moderate expected neuro/metabolic ARR; mostly indirect through sleep, BP, glycemia and function
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Behavioral sleep interventions, consistent sleep scheduling, stimulus control, and CBT-I principles are standard approaches for insufficient or inefficient sleep.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Sleep efficiency was poor enough to fire a neuro modifier, and sleep duration is short on work nights and by wearable average.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Use a structured stress-reduction or behavioral health intervention
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small expected neuro/metabolic ARR; indirect through stress, sleep, BP, alcohol and adherence
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Structured behavioral interventions such as CBT-based stress management, mindfulness-based stress reduction, relaxation training, and coaching are commonly used to reduce stress symptoms and improve sleep and self-management behaviors.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Karen reports high perceived stress during a recent reorganization and family transition, with PSS-10 in the moderate-to-high range and mild anxiety symptoms.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Adopt kidney-protective medication and dehydration safety practices
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small expected renal ARR because current CKD risk is low; high safety value for ACE-inhibitor/dehydration contexts
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Avoiding chronic nephrotoxic medication exposure and preventing dehydration-related AKI are standard kidney-protection practices, especially in people with hypertension, ACE inhibitor use, or metabolic risk.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Current kidney markers are reassuring, but hypertension and prediabetes create future vulnerability.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Correct borderline vitamin D insufficiency and ensure adequate calcium intake
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small prevention-general ARR; mainly bone-health optimization after DEXA/vitamin D context
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Maintaining adequate vitamin D and calcium intake is standard bone-health risk reduction, especially after menopause.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Vitamin D is below many commonly used sufficiency targets, and she is postmenopausal.

SoC Risk Reduction.title: Ensure levothyroxine is taken separately from mineral-containing supplements
SoC Risk Reduction.expected_domain_arr: small prevention-general ARR; medication-effectiveness/safety optimization rather than risk-event reduction
SoC Risk Reduction.grounding_note: Standard medication counseling for levothyroxine includes consistent administration and separation from calcium, iron, magnesium, and multivalent cation supplements because they can impair absorption.
SoC Risk Reduction.rationale: Her thyroid labs are currently controlled, but she takes magnesium and a multivitamin, which may contain minerals that can reduce levothyroxine absorption if taken too close together.

Adjunct Options.title: Structured post-meal walking after higher-carbohydrate meals
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human biomarker studies show that light activity soon after meals can reduce postprandial glucose and insulin exposure.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Add brief walking bouts after meals most likely to raise glucose, especially dinner or takeout meals.

Adjunct Options.title: Short-term continuous glucose monitor experiment for post-meal pattern discovery
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human biomarker evidence supports that postprandial glucose responses vary between individuals and can be modified by meal composition, timing, sleep, and activity.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Use a time-limited CGM trial as an optional risk-clarification tool to identify which meals, timing patterns, stress days, sleep-short nights, or alcohol-containing dinners are associated with larger glucose excursions.

Adjunct Options.title: Meal-sequencing experiment: vegetables and protein before starch
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Small human feeding and biomarker studies suggest that consuming fiber-rich vegetables and protein before carbohydrate can reduce postprandial glucose and insulin spikes.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Test a low-burden meal-order strategy at meals containing refined or higher-glycemic carbohydrates.

Adjunct Options.title: Resistance-training microcycle focused on postmenopausal central adiposity and insulin sensitivity
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human intervention evidence supports resistance training effects on lean mass, insulin sensitivity, functional reserve, and waist measures.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Add a specific, progressive resistance-training block as an exercise-dose optimization candidate, separate from general physical-activity advice.

Adjunct Options.title: Viscous soluble fiber add-on, such as psyllium, if tolerated
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human biomarker evidence supports modest LDL-C lowering and improved postprandial glycemic response with viscous soluble fibers.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Consider a food-or-supplement soluble fiber intervention focused on LDL/non-HDL and postprandial glycemia.

Adjunct Options.title: Portfolio-style food substitution focused on plant sterols, viscous fiber, nuts, and soy protein
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human dietary-intervention evidence shows that combinations of cholesterol-lowering foods can improve LDL-related biomarkers.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Use a defined food-substitution pattern as an optional LDL/ApoB-lowering nutrition adjunct.

Adjunct Options.title: Nitrate-rich vegetable or beetroot trial for BP and exercise-response biomarkers
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human biomarker studies show that dietary nitrate can modestly influence nitric-oxide pathways, BP, and exercise efficiency in some populations.
Adjunct Options.rationale: A short, monitored trial of nitrate-rich foods could be considered for borderline-above-optimal BP and low CRF.

Adjunct Options.title: Higher-intensity interval exposure trial layered onto current cycling if safe and acceptable
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: high (~35% baseline risk × high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human exercise studies show interval training can improve VO2max efficiently in many adults.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Evaluate a cautious interval-training option using her existing indoor cycling habit to improve VO2max.

Adjunct Options.title: Alcohol-free sleep and glucose n-of-1 trial
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human epidemiology and biomarker evidence links alcohol intake with sleep fragmentation, BP, triglycerides, glycemia, and breast cancer risk in dose-dependent ways.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Run a defined alcohol-free or near-alcohol-free trial period with wearable sleep and, if available, glucose markers to see whether sleep efficiency, resting HR, HRV, BP, or next-day glucose patterns improve.

Adjunct Options.title: Digital CBT-I or structured sleep-efficiency protocol
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human evidence supports behavioral sleep interventions for improving sleep efficiency and insomnia symptoms, with downstream biomarker effects on mood, glucose regulation, and BP plausible but less definitive in this exact patient state.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Consider a structured behavioral sleep program to improve sleep efficiency and duration.

Adjunct Options.title: Morning bright-light and evening light-hygiene routine for circadian reinforcement
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human sleep and circadian biomarker evidence supports light timing as a regulator of circadian phase and sleep quality.
Adjunct Options.rationale: A low-downside circadian intervention could be tested to support earlier sleep consolidation and improve sleep efficiency.

Adjunct Options.title: HRV biofeedback or paced-breathing stress-recovery practice
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human biomarker studies suggest paced breathing and HRV biofeedback can influence autonomic measures, perceived stress, and sometimes BP.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Use a monitorable autonomic-regulation practice given high perceived stress, mild anxiety symptoms, and low-normal wearable HRV.

Adjunct Options.title: Food-first omega-3 assessment pathway with optional omega-3 index testing
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human biomarker evidence links omega-3 status to triglyceride and inflammatory pathways, but routine omega-3 index testing and supplement decisions for primary prevention are not universally standard-of-care.
Adjunct Options.rationale: Consider measuring omega-3 index only if it would change behavior, such as increasing fatty fish intake or considering supplementation.

Adjunct Options.title: Sauna or passive heat exposure protocol with BP precautions
Adjunct Options.domain_opportunity: moderate-high (~7.5% baseline risk × moderate-high residual opportunity)
Adjunct Options.grounding_note: Human epidemiologic and physiologic evidence associates sauna use with vascular, BP, and stress-response markers, but causality and patient-specific outcome benefit are uncertain.
Adjunct Options.rationale: A carefully monitored passive heat exposure option could be considered if she is interested and has no contraindicating symptoms.

Adjunct Options.title: H. pylori noninvasive testing as optional gastric-risk clarification

Current kidney markers are normal; opportunity is mainly safety and prevention of downstream risk conversion.

Intermediate near-term vascular risk with multiple guideline-grade modifiable levers.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Routine ovarian cancer screening with CA-125 and transvaginal ultrasound
Risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy solely for ovarian cancer family history
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Exclude unless new genetic or specialist risk information changes eligibility.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Whole-body MRI as broad cancer or longevity screening
Multi-cancer early detection blood test as a substitute for indicated screening
Amyloid PET, brain MRI, or plasma neurodegeneration biomarkers for asymptomatic dementia screening
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Exclude as research-only or non-actionable in this payload.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: Carotid intima-media thickness screening as a CVD reclassifier
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Exclude or deprioritize compared with better-supported CVD risk clarification.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: GLP-1 receptor agonist solely for prediabetes in normal-BMI patient
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Exclude as a non-indicated medication based on supplied data.

Excluded / Watchlist.title: SGLT2 inhibitor for kidney or cardiovascular prevention without diabetes, CKD, or heart failure
Excluded / Watchlist.reason: Exclude as non-indicated from the supplied facts.

input_sufficiency.notes: Used existing Karen v0.9 generated candidate artifact plus Karen domain-risk summary; legacy scalar ranking was not used. Symptom-derived items are not promoted from resolved/improved trajectories; Karen active lanes are driven by objective risk data and current active symptoms.

ranking_run.ranking_architecture: domain-first: order domains by patient-specific absolute opportunity; order actions within domain by expected effect; effort/urgency are metadata
